jobs.title: Head of Internal Audit
jobs.description:
Job description 
 Head of Internal Audit, UBP UK 
 
 

 NAME: 
 
 
 CORPORATE JOB TITLE: 
 Head of Internal Audit, London 
 
 CORPORATE TITLE: 
 TBC 
 
 REPORTS TO: 
 CEO, UBP UK & 
 Group Head of Internal Audit & 
 Head of the Audit Committee (Member of the Board of Directors) 
 
 SENIOR MANAGEMENT FUNCTION (YES/NO): 
 Yes 
 
 PRESCRIBED RESPONSBILITY: 
 SMF 5 
 
 MATERIAL RISK TAKER (YES/NO): 
 Yes 
 
 CERTIFIED (YES/NO): 
 No 
 
 CERTIFIED FUNCTIONS: 
 
 
 
 LAST UPDATED: 
 March 2026 
 
 Main Responsibilities 
 This role will hold overall responsibility for Internal Audit across the UBP UK, providing risk-based objective assurance, advice and insight. UBP Internal Audit follows the standards of the profession, as well as the regulatory requirements and UBP Group policies and procedures. 
 The Head of Internal Audit is responsible for independent and objective audits reviews of all UBP UK activities, aiming at providing the Group with assurance as to how effectively it is controlling its operations, advises on improvements. The role also assesses the quality of risk management within an audited scope, the appropriateness and effectiveness of the permanent control framework, the risk sensitivity of management and compliance with codes of conduct and expected professional practices. 
 Role Duties 
 The Head of Audit is responsible for managing, planning, and administering the internal audit function in the UK, Channel Islands and Gibraltar regions for all activities of UBP UK. 
 Plan, supervise and take part in audits across all activities of UBP UK including financial, regulatory, compliance, operational audits as needed. 
 Develop and maintain good relationships with the head and key management personnel of all activity pillars across UBP UK. 
 Make recommendations for organisational and internal controls improvements of UBP UK. 
 Prepare Audit reports, and summaries of findings and audit reports and recommendations to the Group Audit department, as well as key management stakeholders of UBP UK. 
 Follow-up of Audit Recommendations until their implementation. 
 Make recommendations for improving the efficiency and effectiveness of the audit process. 
 Managing and engaging with various stakeholders across all departments of UBP UK at a senior level. 
 Liaise with external auditors and regulators. 
 
 
 The job description is not exhaustive and may include other duties as assigned from time to time by management. 
 
 Management and Supervision 
 Current line management responsibility for one Internal Audit Manager. 
 Set goals and objectives for all staff under direct supervision, complete performance appraisals and arrange appraisal interviews and ensure that the UBP UK guidelines are appropriately updated and communicated to staff. 
 
 Statements of Responsibility 
 Prescribed Responsibilities and Local Responsibilities for the Head of Internal Audit are set out in the Statements of Responsibility which is maintained as a separate document. 
 
 Conduct Rules 
 This role is required to comply with the Financial Conduct Authority’s (FCA) Individual Conduct Rules, which are: 
 
 To act with integrity; 
 To act with due skill, care and diligence; 
 To be open and cooperative with the FCA, the Prudential Regulation Authoritity (PRA) and other regulators; 
 To pay due regard to the interests of customers and treat them fairly; 
 To observe proper standards of market conduct; and 
 To act to deliver good outcomes for retail customers. 
 
 In addition, this role must also comply with the Senior Manager Conduct Rules, which are: 
 
 To take reasonable steps to ensure that the business of the firm for which you are responsible is controlled effectively. 
 To take reasonable steps to ensure that the business of the firm for which you are responsible complies with the relevant requirements and standards of the regulatory system. 
 To take reasonable steps to ensure that any delegation of your responsibilities is to an appropriate person and that you oversee the discharge of the delegated responsibility effectively. 
 To disclose appropriately any information of which the FCA or PRA would reasonably expect notice. 
 
 Person Specification 
 Knowledge, Skills & Experience 
 
 Qualifications 
 Qualifications: Professional Audit/Accounting qualification or Degree is preferred (ACA, ACCA, CIA, etc.) 
 Good knowledge of accounting principles and professional audit standards. 
 Solid experience at a Head of Internal Audit or Senior Audit Manager level with an audit firm or a financial institution. 
 Working experience in both wealth management (essential) and asset management industries (preferred). 
 Experience managing audits across a broad range of activities, including financial, compliance, regulatory, operational, systems, etc. 
 Able to influence senior stakeholders whilst maintaining objectivity and independence 
 Written skills for preparing and delivering quality audit reports, as well as strong relationship building and communication skills to ensure the best outcome. 
 Knowledge of UK banking legislation, regulations and rules (PRA/FCA) 
 Excellent communicator and able to build relationships with internal and external stakeholders including regulators 
 Ability to influence senior stakeholders 
 Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to assess complex situations and make sound decisions. 
 Demonstrated experience in developing and implementing compliance policies, and procedures. 
 On-going CPD (Continuous Professional Development) to ensure that adequate knowledge of the UK regulatory environment is maintained. 
 Suitably qualified to hold SMF 5. 
 
 Competencies and Values Required Level 
 
 Shows the drive to perform and be successful 5 
 Persistent in following-up and through on issues 5 
 Upholds the legal, regulatory and ethical standard of the UBP Group 5 
 Good product knowledge 4 
 Knowledge of legal regulatory and industry requirements 5 
 Business Development 3 
 Communication - listening, written and verbal with clients 5 
 Influencing 5 
 Relationship Building - internal and external 5 
 Strategic & commercial awareness 4 
 Planning & organising 4 
 Ethics and values 5 
 Customer focus 5 
 Explanation: 1: low level of competence / skill required, 2-3: average level of competence / skill required, 4-5: high level of competence skill required

 Good knowledge of accounting principles and professional audit standards.

 Working experience in both wealth management (essential) and asset management industries (preferred). 
 Experience managing audits across a broad range of activities, including financial, compliance, regulatory, operational, systems, etc. 
 Able to influence senior stakeholders whilst maintaining objectivity and independence 
 Written skills for preparing and delivering quality audit reports, as well as strong relationship building and communication skills to ensure the best outcome.

 Excellent communicator and able to build relationships with internal and external stakeholders including regulators 
 Ability to influence senior stakeholders 
 Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to assess complex situations and make sound decisions. 
 Demonstrated experience in developing and implementing compliance policies, and procedures. 
 On-going CPD (Continuous Professional Development) to ensure that adequate knowledge of the UK regulatory environment is maintained. 
 Suitably qualified to hold SMF 5. 
 
 Competencies and Values Required Level 
 
 Shows the drive to perform and be successful 5 
 Persistent in following-up and through on issues 5 
 Upholds the legal, regulatory and ethical standard of the UBP Group 5 
 Good product knowledge 4 
 Knowledge of legal regulatory and industry requirements 5

 Develop and maintain good relationships with the head and key management personnel of all activity pillars across UBP UK. 
 Make recommendations for organisational and internal controls improvements of UBP UK. 
 Prepare Audit reports, and summaries of findings and audit reports and recommendations to the Group Audit department, as well as key management stakeholders of UBP UK. 
 Follow-up of Audit Recommendations until their implementation. 
 Make recommendations for improving the efficiency and effectiveness of the audit process. 
 Managing and engaging with various stakeholders across all departments of UBP UK at a senior level. 
 Liaise with external auditors and regulators. 
 
 
 The job description is not exhaustive and may include other duties as assigned from time to time by management. 
 
 Management and Supervision 
 Current line management responsibility for one Internal Audit Manager. 
 Set goals and objectives for all staff under direct supervision, complete performance appraisals and arrange appraisal interviews and ensure that the UBP UK guidelines are appropriately updated and communicated to staff. 
 
 Statements of Responsibility 
 Prescribed Responsibilities and Local Responsibilities for the Head of Internal Audit are set out in the Statements of Responsibility which is maintained as a separate document. 
 
 Conduct Rules 
 This role is required to comply with the Financial Conduct Authority’s (FCA) Individual Conduct Rules, which are: 
 
 To act with integrity; 
 To act with due skill, care and diligence; 
 To be open and cooperative with the FCA, the Prudential Regulation Authoritity (PRA) and other regulators; 
 To pay due regard to the interests of customers and treat them fairly; 
 To observe proper standards of market conduct; and 
 To act to deliver good outcomes for retail customers. 
 
 In addition, this role must also comply with the Senior Manager Conduct Rules, which are: 
 
 To take reasonable steps to ensure that the business of the firm for which you are responsible is controlled effectively. 
 To take reasonable steps to ensure that the business of the firm for which you are responsible complies with the relevant requirements and standards of the regulatory system. 
 To take reasonable steps to ensure that any delegation of your responsibilities is to an appropriate person and that you oversee the discharge of the delegated responsibility effectively. 
 To disclose appropriately any information of which the FCA or PRA would reasonably expect notice. 
 
 Person Specification 
 Knowledge, Skills & Experience 
 
 Qualifications 
 Qualifications: Professional Audit/Accounting qualification or Degree is preferred (ACA, ACCA, CIA, etc.) 
 Good knowledge of accounting principles and professional audit standards. 
 Solid experience at a Head of Internal Audit or Senior Audit Manager level with an audit firm or a financial institution. 
 Working experience in both wealth management (essential) and asset management industries (preferred). 
 Experience managing audits across a broad range of activities, including financial, compliance, regulatory, operational, systems, etc. 
 Able to influence senior stakeholders whilst maintaining objectivity and independence 
 Written skills for preparing and delivering quality audit reports, as well as strong relationship building and communication skills to ensure the best outcome. 
 Knowledge of UK banking legislation, regulations and rules (PRA/FCA) 
 Excellent communicator and able to build relationships with internal and external stakeholders including regulators 
 Ability to influence senior stakeholders 
 Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to assess complex situations and make sound decisions. 
 Demonstrated experience in developing and implementing compliance policies, and procedures. 
 On-going CPD (Continuous Professional Development) to ensure that adequate knowledge of the UK regulatory environment is maintained. 
 Suitably qualified to hold SMF 5. 
 
 Competencies and Values Required Level 
 
 Shows the drive to perform and be successful 5 
 Persistent in following-up and through on issues 5 
 Upholds the legal, regulatory and ethical standard of the UBP Group 5 
 Good product knowledge 4 
 Knowledge of legal regulatory and industry requirements 5 
 Business Development 3 
 Communication - listening, written and verbal with clients 5 
 Influencing 5 
 Relationship Building - internal and external 5 
 Strategic & commercial awareness 4 
 Planning & organising 4 
 Ethics and values 5 
 Customer focus 5 
 Explanation: 1: low level of competence / skill required, 2-3: average level of competence / skill required, 4-5: high level of competence skill required

The role also assesses the quality of risk management within an audited scope, the appropriateness and effectiveness of the permanent control framework, the risk sensitivity of management and compliance with codes of conduct and expected professional practices.

jobs.title: WM Cross-Border Specialist (9 months contract)
jobs.description:
Mission 
 The Business Monitoring team is one of the most transversal activities within Wealth Management (WM). In this team, your role will be to bring support and expertise to WM function in all aspects relating to cross border regulatory framework. 
 
 Main responsibilities 
 Ensure full availability of country manuals and e-learnings according to markets covered by the Wealth Management & Asset Management activities 
 Ensure timely completion of trainings by WM Front Office 
 Coordinate trainings with the support of Learning & Development team 
 Manage the relationship with the external Country Manuals provider 
 Ensure appropriate adaptation of provider’s manuals to the Bank’s Risk Appetite and strategy 
 Maintain and develop the Cross Border’s control framework in adequation with the overall control framework (investment and banking services restrictions, sanctions, etc) 
 Maintain and develop the MIS and reporting around Cross Border control framework and activities 
 Develop and perform control activities around Cross Border framework (thematic reviews, business trips, investments, conduct,…) 
 Any other task required by Management in the context of the employee’s main mission 
 

 Your Profile 
 Degree in a related field (bachelor in Finance, banking apprenticeship, CYP, banking internship or similar) 
 High proficiency in MS Office including Excel & Powerpoint 
 Knowledge in Power BI, SQL, Power Query, Business Object are a plus 
 Minimum 3 years of experience in Cross Border management in a Swiss Private Bank 
 Proficiency in Compliance and Regulatory matters surrounding WM business in Switzerland (FinSA) 
 Strong analytical, organizational and research skills as well as curious & open mindset, flexibility, team-player and goal oriented 
 Fluent in French and English. Any additional language is a plus 
 
 Others 
 Swiss Residence 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Facilities Officer (Temporary)
jobs.description:
Mission 
 Veiller à la maintenance et à la sécurité des locaux tout en assurant le bien-être de leurs occupants.
Capacité à trouver des solutions efficaces et pragmatiques répondant aux besoins techniques des utilisateurs
 
 Main responsibilities Répondre aux demandes des collaborateurs sur les problématiques liées aux locaux et équipements.
 Effectuer les petites réparations et interventions de maintenance de premier niveau.
 Contrôler l'état général des bâtiments et signaler les anomalies.
 Coordonner et accompagner les prestataires lors des interventions en s'assurant du respect des règles de sécurité de la banque.
 Vérifier visuellement les installations techniques et remonter les différents problèmes.
 Participer à l'organisation des espaces de travail.
 Effectuer des déménagements internes et des mises en place de mobiliers.
 Participer à la gestion des stocks de fournitures et passer les commandes validées par le responsable.
 Vérifier le bon fonctionnement des équipements de sécurité.
 Appliquer les consignes de sécurité de la banque.
 Participer au piquet technique.
 
 
 Your Profile Expérience attendue de Facilities officer en interne ou dans une société de service. 
 Connaissances en bâtiment. 
o Electricité : vérification des installations et relamping. 
o Plomberie : Débouchage de canalisations simples et entretien des sanitaires. 
o Serrurerie/Menuiserie : Ajustement portes des fenêtres et du mobilier, changement des serrures. 
o Revêtement : remplacement de revêtement sols.
 Polyvalence et autonomie dans l'exécution des tâches. 
 Réactivité et capacité à gérer les urgences. 
 Sens du service et excellent relationnel. 
 Rigueur et respect des consignes. 
 
 
 Background High School/ Maturité / Baccalauréat 
 Connaissances en sécurité incendie et accessibilité des bâtiments. 
 Excel, GMAO, Ticketing. 
 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Graduate Programme - Operations office team
jobs.description:
The Bank’s entire operations are managed by COO Division and are headed up by the Chief Operating Officer. It strives to provide both in-house and external clients with a first-rate service to ensure our business operates efficiently and at the best possible cost. The COO division comprises Operations, Finance, Information Management, Human Resources and Logistics. COO employees are committed to providing, with professionalism and enthusiasm, an efficient, proactive, high-quality service that meets the needs of clients within the context of the Bank’s overall strategy. 
 Mission : 
 Permanent contract and training programme of 12 months, based on a rotation through various departments of the COO team 

 The purpose is to acquire targeted professional skills as well as practical experience 

 You will learn to manage projects, build stakeholder relationships, and conduct change management 

 You will be supported by a mentor, and a “buddy” during the entire programme 

 This programme is dedicated to young graduates, with a Master’s degree and a maximum of 12 months’ global professional experience, seeking to discover the Banking industry 

 It includes the prospect of obtaining a permanent role within UBP Operations Team 
 

 Main responsibilities : 
 1. Operations – Monitoring and Reconciliations 
 Understand the role of the Operations department, and the roles of the different teams within Operations 

 Understand Operations processes and propose process improvements where applicable 

 Participate in coordination tasks of transversal topics / projects within Operations department 

 Help to realise reportings and collect inputs from other teams on regular reportings 

 Understand and support Reconciliation’s activity and investigations 
 

 2. Banking Operations 
 Join an Operations team 

 Understand the role of the Operations department, and the roles of the different teams within Operations 

 Understand Operations processes and propose process improvements where applicable 

 Support the processes of the teams in line with internal procedures and policies 

 Support the cash services and securities services by discovering transfers, treasury operations, stock exchange and settlement activities. 

 
 3. Risk management department 
 Join the risk management team 

 Understand the different roles within the risk team 

 Understand Operational risk going through incident management, RCSA… 

 Support risk processes 

 
 Your Profile : 
 Master’s degree in finance or Information Technology obtained recently at a highly recognised university 

 Maximum 12 months’ professional experience 

 Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, ability to partner with other teams 

 Fluent in French and English; any other language would be a plus 

 Excellent knowledge of the Microsoft Office suite especially Excel, programming skills is a plus 

 Strong critical thinking, analytical and problem-solving skills 

 Team player 

 
 Others : Swiss Residence

 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

descriptionByLocale.en:
The Bank’s entire operations are managed by COO Division and are headed up by the Chief Operating Officer. It strives to provide both in-house and external clients with a first-rate service to ensure our business operates efficiently and at the best possible cost. The COO division comprises Operations, Finance, Information Management, Human Resources and Logistics. COO employees are committed to providing, with professionalism and enthusiasm, an efficient, proactive, high-quality service that meets the needs of clients within the context of the Bank’s overall strategy. 
 Mission : 
 Permanent contract and training programme of 12 months, based on a rotation through various departments of the COO team 

 The purpose is to acquire targeted professional skills as well as practical experience 

 You will learn to manage projects, build stakeholder relationships, and conduct change management 

 You will be supported by a mentor, and a “buddy” during the entire programme 

 This programme is dedicated to young graduates, with a Master’s degree and a maximum of 12 months’ global professional experience, seeking to discover the Banking industry 

 It includes the prospect of obtaining a permanent role within UBP Operations Team 
 

 Main responsibilities : 
 1. Operations – Monitoring and Reconciliations 
 Understand the role of the Operations department, and the roles of the different teams within Operations 

 Understand Operations processes and propose process improvements where applicable 

 Participate in coordination tasks of transversal topics / projects within Operations department 

 Help to realise reportings and collect inputs from other teams on regular reportings 

 Understand and support Reconciliation’s activity and investigations 
 

 2. Banking Operations 
 Join an Operations team 

 Understand the role of the Operations department, and the roles of the different teams within Operations 

 Understand Operations processes and propose process improvements where applicable 

 Support the processes of the teams in line with internal procedures and policies 

 Support the cash services and securities services by discovering transfers, treasury operations, stock exchange and settlement activities. 

 
 3. Risk management department 
 Join the risk management team 

 Understand the different roles within the risk team 

 Understand Operational risk going through incident management, RCSA… 

 Support risk processes 

 
 Your Profile : 
 Master’s degree in finance or Information Technology obtained recently at a highly recognised university 

 Maximum 12 months’ professional experience 

 Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, ability to partner with other teams 

 Fluent in French and English; any other language would be a plus 

 Excellent knowledge of the Microsoft Office suite especially Excel, programming skills is a plus 

 Strong critical thinking, analytical and problem-solving skills 

 Team player 

 
 Others : Swiss Residence
 
 
 Applications : 
 Applications must be made through our careers website and must include a CV and a cover letter. 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: Graduate Programme – Group Risk Management team

titleByLocale.en: Graduate Programme – Group Risk Management team
titleByLocale.fr: Graduate Programme – Group Risk Management team
titleByLocale.it: Graduate Programme – Group Risk Management team
titleByLocale.de: Graduate Programme – Group Risk Management team

jobs.description:
The risk management activities with the Group are supervised by Group Risk Management, headed up by the Group Head of Risk Management (“CRO”). Risk Management Teams located in Switzerland are in charge to oversight the Bank activities in Switzerland and to coordinate methodologies, deploy transversal risk activities and key projects with Local Risk Management Representatives at Group level. In Switzerland, Teams are composed by Treasury and Trading Team, Asset Management Team, Wealth Management Team and Operational Risk Team, while a project Team provide a transversal support on key projects or initiatives leaded by CRO or Team Heads. Acting as 2nd line of defence, Teams all provide advisory to Business, participate to local Committees, perform controls, challenge and support risk assessments and their response plans in coordination with Risk Owners. 
 
 Mission 
 
As of 1 September 2026, Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) is offering a permanent position within its Group Risk Management teams (2nd line of defence) in Geneva, starting with a 12‑month training program that includes regular interactions with other Bank functions and Legal Entity representatives across the Group.
The purpose is to acquire targeted professional skills as well as practical experience with a strong orientation on risk management and secondly on project management.
 Rotations 
 The program consists of four three month rotations across the following teams: 
 Group Risk Management Project Team
 Wealth Management Team, 1st Line of Defence 
 Risk Management Team, 2nd Line of Defence (Luxembourg) 
 Bank’s Change Management Team (Transversal Projects), part of the Group COO teams
 
 
 Main responsibilities 
 During the program, under the supervision of the team managers in their role of mentor, the Graduate is expected to provide support on all teams’ activities as well as assist them in their daily tasks, with in particular:
 Participate and perform to Risk assessments and Control assessment exercises across the Bank / the location.
 Suggest risk and/or control methodologies enhancements.
 Perform control activities and present outcome internally.
 Participate to projects, in cooperation with teams and Subject-Matter-Experts.
 Participate and perform thematic reviews and formulate enhancement opportunities.
 Prepare reporting to Senior Management.
 
 
 During the program, you will also have the possibility to start a certification related to Banking sector and/or your function, for examples the CFA (Chartered Financial Analyst) or CIA (Certified Internal Auditor), further enhancing your professional development.
 
 Your Profile 
 Background 
 Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in finance, business management recently obtained from a highly recognised university or equivalent.
 Maximum 12-months’ professional experience.
 Knowledges 
 Good understanding of banking / financial sector, interest for Private Banking model and challenge / regulations.
 Knowledge of risk management and/or control activities an expectation. 
 Experience in project management a plus.
 Strong analytical skills, strong presentation skills.
 Technical Skills 
 Excellent knowledge of the Microsoft Office tools especially Excel, PowerPoint.
 Knowledge in Power BI a plus.
 Fluent in English and French (minimum C1 in both).
 Specific Competencies 
 High motivation, attention to details, excellent organization skills and strong attention to deadline 
 Discretion as a need-to-have
 Multi-tasking and solutions oriented
 
 Very strong team spirit and capable to work independently
 
 Candidate must hold a valid work permit or be eligible to work in the locations 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives. 
 
 Applications 
 Applications must include a CV, diploma(s) and a cover letter.

jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: Stock exchange / Settlement Officer
jobs.description:
TEMPORARY MISSION - 9 MONTHS 
 
 Mission : 
 

 Settlement of equities, bonds, structured products​ and investment funds transactions. 
 
 

 Main duties : 

 Monitoring of pending transactions. 

 Control and monitoring positions. 

 Contact with traders, custodians and counterparties. 

 Respect the process and its implementation in relation to operational requirements and volumes as directed by the bank. 
 
 

 Core competencies : 
 
 • Think in "Problem-Action-Results" terms 
 • Show strong client service ethic 
 • Be proactive and driven 
 • Express opinions and criticism in a constructive way 
 • Be a continuous learner 
 • Adopt change with a positive attitude 
 • Consistently comply with internal & external regulations 
 • Go beyond specific assignments, walk the extra mile 
 • Seek opportunities to improve the existing 
 • Be open to other's idea's 
 • Balance personal and team interests 
 • Seek opportunities to improve quality in all features of the work environment 
 • Maintain quality standards despite schedule pressures 

 
 Specific Competencies : 

 The complete flow of a transaction 

 The parties involved on the market (counterparties, custodians, clearing, companies) 

 Financial instruments (equities, bonds, structured products, investment funds) 

 Good management priorities and ability to deal with sensitive situations 

 
 Other Skills/Abilities : 

 CFC bank or equivalent 

 Ability to work within a team 

 French/English 

 MS Office / banking software / Swift 

 Knowledge of Omgeo and Euclid is a plus 

 
 Others : 
 Swiss Residence 
 

 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.description:
Mission 
 Through the Asset Management division, we offer to institutional clients’ investment management capabilities organised across the business lines Long-Only Platform, Alternative Investment Solutions, Private Markets Group, Partnerships, Asset Allocation & DPM. 
 The Institutional Client Service Team serves as the primary contact for large and complex institutional clients globally, supporting sales teams in engaging institutional and wholesale clients. The team plays a vital role in enhancing profitability and driving growth within the Asset Management business. 
 We are seeking a dynamic candidate with a strong interest in Asset Management to join the Institutional Client Service team in Frankfurt . This role involves close collaboration with the German sales team, reporting locally to the Head of Wholesale EMEA and centrally to the COO - Institutional Clients & Head of the Client Service Team. The position covers activities with both institutional and wholesale clients in the DACH region (Germany, German-speaking Switzerland, and Austria) This position is for 12 months, but it may be renewed or offer other opportunities. 
 
 Main responsibilities 
 Assist Asset Management Sales teams in supporting German-speaking client-base. 

 Manage, execute, and follow up on client requests and inquiries. 

 Collaborate with internal teams throughout the client lifecycle to ensure client satisfaction and retention. 

 Coordinate the onboarding process and oversee the end-to-end transition. 

 Involve in the implementation and monitoring of the local sales strategy. 

 Support the team in their prospecting initiatives, including the preparation of marketing materials and conducting market research. 

 Contribute to the optimization of processes through the application of AI technologies. 

 Act as the local CRM champion, supporting and training the sales team to meet Salesforce requirements and ensure data accuracy. 

 Organise local events and roadshows. 

 Perform office management tasks. 

 
 Your Profile 
 Bachelor’s degree in business administration, Marketing, or a related field is beneficial. 

 Excellent organizational skills and capability to work independently. 

 Accuracy and attention to detail. 

 Advanced skills in standard IT applications (MS Office). 

 Mother tongue German and fluency in English is a “MUST”; French is a “nice to have”. 

 
 Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) is a family-owned company, driven by an entrepreneurial vision, innovation and independence. UBP is one of the most strongly-capitalised private banks. 
 By joining UBP Asset Management (Europe) S.A. you will embrace a team of supportive high qualified professionals in Private Banking that will lead you to develop your career to the top level. We encourage continuous learning, development and knowledge sharing. We foster a culture of Agility, Conviction, Dedication and high degree of Responsibility. 
 UBP Asset Management (Europe) S.A encourages all suitable qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity or expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity. 
 In accordance with the provisions of the Luxembourg law of 23 July 2016 amending the law of 29 March 2013, retained candidate will be required to provide an extract from their criminal record as a part of UBP screening process.

jobs.title: Client Documentation Specialist
jobs.description:
Mission 
 
 As part of the Client Documentation team, the Client Documentation Specialist is a key element to ensure:
 A smooth documents process flow between the different departments of the bank, especially regarding change in circumstances 
 The quality, coherence and the accuracy of the client's data in the bank's systems 
 The relevance of clients' instructions by performing control signature
 An efficient support to the other departments of the bank and the branches
 

 Main responsibilities 
 
 Day to day business 
 
 Review the documentation received from the clients for certain change in circumstances on an existing account or an account closing and verify that it complies with the local and international regulations (e.g. CDB20, Common Reporting Standards (CRS) and FATCA/QI rules) and the Bank's directives and procedures from (i) a formal point of view (e.g. the correct version of the document is signed, all the required fields have been completed) and (ii) that the information is consistent across the documents provided by the clients (IDs, corporate documents, bank's forms, etc…)
 Verify that the information entered in the Bank's systems by the Wealth Management is complete according to the documentation signed
 Perform 4 eyes controls and regular internal controls to ensure respect of the procedures 
 Ensure a good quality of service by providing accurate feedback to the Wealth Management on any error, missing document and answering in a timely manner to any query related to his/her mission and duties
 
 Coordination and central point of contact 
 Build a bridge and create synergy within the Client Documentation team and other key players (Legal, Compliance, IT…), create/maintain an efficient flow of communication that ensures all remain aware of important information and that it is consistent
 Act as a business partner by bringing his/her expertise and the relevant support and input to the other departments of the bank (Wealth Management, Compliance, Legal…) when required (on a complex situation, on the implementation of new regulations or bank's decisions, new projects)
 Coordinate efforts to help a smooth flow in the processes and always seek for improvements
 Facilitate problem solving and collaboration
 Create an environment oriented to trust, open communication and cohesive team effort
 
 Projects and Change management 
 Be up to date on any changes in the regulations, perform the assessment for internal changes, communicate with the relevant departments involved and to the team, implement them or launch projects, requests to IT as needed
 Participate in any project impacting the team, either required by a new regulation or initiated by the Bank, hence participate to workshops, meetings, upon the management delegation (for defining the business requirements, performing the testings, and any other required tasks)
 Involve the other team members as required
 Participate in the change management within the team by communicating proactively on the various projects and accompany the team in the change 
 Make sure that the procedures and processes are updated accordingly and communicated to all stakeholders, including the Client Documentation teams within the different branches.
 
 
 Your Profile 
 

 Education: Bachelors Degree
 Experience Technical skills: At ease with Microsoft tools, especially Excel
 Languages: English is a requirement, German/Italian or other language is a plus 
 Personal skills : Client service oriented, Curiosity and good interpersonal skills
 Swiss Residence mandatory 
 
 Core Competencies : 
 Open mind and team orientation: Be open to other's ideas. / Balance personal & team interests. / Express opinion and criticism in a constructive way.
 Innovation and development: Seek opportunities to improve the existing. / Be a continuous learner. / Adopt change with a positive attitude.
 Professional business culture: Think in 'Problem-Action-Results' terms. / Walk the extra mile. / Be proactive and driven. / Show strong client service ethic. / Build lasting client relationships.
 Quality, internal & external regulations: Consistently comply with internal & external regulations. / Seek opportunities to improve quality in all features of the work environment.
 Analytical Ability : Ability to assimilate procedures and to implement them. / Ability to identify issues and raise them in a structured way. / Ability to make process improvements. / Attention to detail.
 Solid organization and time management skills: Ability to organize the tasks efficiently based on priorities with anticipation of events. / Reaction and decision-making ability in face of unforeseen circumstances.
 Communication: Ability to raise issues via the right channel and in a constructive way. / Ability to expose constraints and opportunities. / Express opinions and criticism in a constructive way.
 Adaptability: Ability to change, be flexible. / Maintain quality standards despite schedule pressures.
 Team Spirit : Ability to proactively help the others. / Ability to finalize the common goal.

 Professional business culture: Think in 'Problem-Action-Results' terms. / Walk the extra mile. / Be proactive and driven. / Show strong client service ethic. / Build lasting client relationships.
 Quality, internal & external regulations: Consistently comply with internal & external regulations. / Seek opportunities to improve quality in all features of the work environment.
 Analytical Ability : Ability to assimilate procedures and to implement them. / Ability to identify issues and raise them in a structured way. / Ability to make process improvements. / Attention to detail.
 Solid organization and time management skills: Ability to organize the tasks efficiently based on priorities with anticipation of events. / Reaction and decision-making ability in face of unforeseen circumstances.
 Communication: Ability to raise issues via the right channel and in a constructive way. / Ability to expose constraints and opportunities. / Express opinions and criticism in a constructive way.
 Adaptability: Ability to change, be flexible. / Maintain quality standards despite schedule pressures.
 Team Spirit : Ability to proactively help the others. / Ability to finalize the common goal.
descriptionByLocale.fr:
Mission 
 
 As part of the Client Documentation team, the Client Documentation Specialist is a key element to ensure:
 A smooth documents process flow between the different departments of the bank, especially regarding change in circumstances 
 The quality, coherence and the accuracy of the client's data in the bank's systems 
 The relevance of clients' instructions by performing control signature
 An efficient support to the other departments of the bank and the branches
 

 Main responsibilities 
 
 Day to day business 
 
 Review the documentation received from the clients for certain change in circumstances on an existing account or an account closing and verify that it complies with the local and international regulations (e.g. CDB20, Common Reporting Standards (CRS) and FATCA/QI rules) and the Bank's directives and procedures from (i) a formal point of view (e.g. the correct version of the document is signed, all the required fields have been completed) and (ii) that the information is consistent across the documents provided by the clients (IDs, corporate documents, bank's forms, etc…)
 Verify that the information entered in the Bank's systems by the Wealth Management is complete according to the documentation signed
 Perform 4 eyes controls and regular internal controls to ensure respect of the procedures 
 Ensure a good quality of service by providing accurate feedback to the Wealth Management on any error, missing document and answering in a timely manner to any query related to his/her mission and duties
 
 Coordination and central point of contact 
 Build a bridge and create synergy within the Client Documentation team and other key players (Legal, Compliance, IT…), create/maintain an efficient flow of communication that ensures all remain aware of important information and that it is consistent
 Act as a business partner by bringing his/her expertise and the relevant support and input to the other departments of the bank (Wealth Management, Compliance, Legal…) when required (on a complex situation, on the implementation of new regulations or bank's decisions, new projects)
 Coordinate efforts to help a smooth flow in the processes and always seek for improvements
 Facilitate problem solving and collaboration
 Create an environment oriented to trust, open communication and cohesive team effort
 
 Projects and Change management 
 Be up to date on any changes in the regulations, perform the assessment for internal changes, communicate with the relevant departments involved and to the team, implement them or launch projects, requests to IT as needed
 Participate in any project impacting the team, either required by a new regulation or initiated by the Bank, hence participate to workshops, meetings, upon the management delegation (for defining the business requirements, performing the testings, and any other required tasks)
 Involve the other team members as required
 Participate in the change management within the team by communicating proactively on the various projects and accompany the team in the change 
 Make sure that the procedures and processes are updated accordingly and communicated to all stakeholders, including the Client Documentation teams within the different branches.
 
 
 Your Profile 
 

 Education: Bachelors Degree
 Experience Technical skills: At ease with Microsoft tools, especially Excel
 Languages: English is a requirement, German/Italian or other language is a plus 
 Personal skills : Client service oriented, Curiosity and good interpersonal skills
 Swiss Residence mandatory 
 
 Core Competencies : 
 Open mind and team orientation: Be open to other's ideas. / Balance personal & team interests. / Express opinion and criticism in a constructive way.
 Innovation and development: Seek opportunities to improve the existing. / Be a continuous learner. / Adopt change with a positive attitude.
 Professional business culture: Think in 'Problem-Action-Results' terms. / Walk the extra mile. / Be proactive and driven. / Show strong client service ethic. / Build lasting client relationships.
 Quality, internal & external regulations: Consistently comply with internal & external regulations. / Seek opportunities to improve quality in all features of the work environment.
 Analytical Ability : Ability to assimilate procedures and to implement them. / Ability to identify issues and raise them in a structured way. / Ability to make process improvements. / Attention to detail.
 Solid organization and time management skills: Ability to organize the tasks efficiently based on priorities with anticipation of events. / Reaction and decision-making ability in face of unforeseen circumstances.
 Communication: Ability to raise issues via the right channel and in a constructive way. / Ability to expose constraints and opportunities. / Express opinions and criticism in a constructive way.
 Adaptability: Ability to change, be flexible. / Maintain quality standards despite schedule pressures.
 Team Spirit : Ability to proactively help the others. / Ability to finalize the common goal.

jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: Senior Legal Advisor - Management Company (M/F)
jobs.description:
Mission 
 As a Senior Legal Advisor specialized in investment funds, you will play a central role within UBP Management Company, UBP Asset Management (Europe) S.A. You will be involved in a broad range of fund related legal and regulatory matters, including regulatory compliance, fund governance, and structuring of new products. 
 You will work closely with all stakeholders of the Management Company, providing legal expertise to support the company’s strategic and operational activities. You will also collaborate daily with our Product Management team and actively contributing to the structuring, management, and evolution of investment products. This key role will allow you to work on diverse projects and collaborate with multidisciplinary teams in a dynamic and international environment. 
 You will also be in charged of the corporate governance of the _Management Company and its branches. 
 
 Your main responsibilities will be: 
 Legal Advice : Provide legal advice on the company’s activities, particularly in corporate law, investment fund law, and financial law. 
 Ensure the company’s activities comply with local and European regulations (CSSF, AIFMD, UCITS, etc.). 
 Draft, review, and negotiate contracts, partnership agreements, management mandates, and other legal documents. 
 
 Collaboration with the Product Management Team: Work closely with the Product Management team on the structuring, implementation, and management of investment products. 
 Provide legal support for the development of new products and the update of existing ones. 
 
 Fund Management: Oversee the creation, structuring, and legal management of investment funds (UCITS, AIF, SICAV, FIS, RAIF, etc.). 
 Prepare and update legal documentation for funds (prospectus, articles of association, management regulations, etc.). 
 Manage relationships with regulatory authorities (CSSF) and follow up on regulatory requests. 
 
 Compliance and Regulatory Monitoring : Conduct legal and regulatory monitoring to anticipate legislative changes and their impact on the company’s activities. 
 Collaborate with the compliance team to ensure adherence to legal and regulatory obligations. 
 
 Litigation Management : Handle potential disputes in collaboration with external law firms. 
 Defend the company’s interests in litigation or judicial proceedings. 
 
 Training and Support: Train and raise awareness among internal teams about regulatory developments and best legal practices. 
 Provide legal support to various departments within the company. 
 
 
 Your Profile: 
 Master’s degree in Business Law, Corporate Law, or Financial Law. 
 Specialization in investment fund law or financial law is an asset. 
 5 to 10 years of experience as a legal counsel in a Management Company, law firm, or financial institution in Luxembourg. 
 Proved experience in managing investment funds (UCITS, AIF, etc.). 
 Excellent knowledge of corporate law, investment fund law, and Luxembourg and European financial regulations (CSSF, AIFMD, UCITS, etc.). 
 Proficiency in the processes of structuring and managing investment funds. 
 Ability to draft and negotiate complex contracts in both French and English. 
 Strong organizational skills and attention to detail. 
 Excellent analytical and synthesis skills. 
 Ability to work independently while effectively collaborating with multidisciplinary teams. 
 Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 
 Discretion and respect for confidentiality (knowledge of German or Luxembourgish is an asset). 
 
 Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) is a family-owned company, driven by an entrepreneurial vision, innovation and independence. UBP is one of the most strongly-capitalised private banks. 
 By joining UBP Asset Management (Europe) S.A. you will embrace a team of supportive high qualified professionals in Private Banking that will lead you to develop your career to the top level. We encourage continuous learning, development and knowledge sharing. We foster a culture of Agility, Conviction, Dedication and high degree of Responsibility. 
 UBP Asset Management (Europe) S.A encourages all suitable qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity or expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity. 
 In accordance with the provisions of the Luxembourg law of 23 July 2016 amending the law of 29 March 2013, retained candidate will be required to provide an extract from their criminal record as a part of UBP screening process.

jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: Internal Auditor
jobs.description:
Mission 
 Reporting to the Group Head of Internal Audit or to their local Heads, Internal Auditors work with Internal Audit Managers to successfully complete recurring audit engagements and special mandates. 
 Main responsibilities Assist in planning the audit work, performing the risk assessment and the engagement objectives setting.
 Perform audit testing in accordance with the audit objectives set by the audit manager.
 Make valid observations on organisational and internal controls matters.
 Prepare the working paper audit file.
 Assist in the preparation of audit reports.
 Assist in the follow-up of Audit observations.
 Invest into continuous learning & development to ensure relevancy of knowledge and skills.
 Contribute to the development and internal promotion of the Internal Audit function, and support innovation.
 
 
 Your Profile At least 3 years of relevant audit experience with an auditing firm, a bank or an asset management company. The position will be adjusted to the seniority of the selected candidate.
 Master in a relevant field.
 Holding relevant professional certifications is a plus (IAP/CIA/CPA/CISA)
 Integrity of character, intellectual curiosity, and strong interpersonal skills
 Organizational and time management skills
 Analytical Thinking – Able to analyze complex information and data and draw conclusions.
 Strong overall risk awareness – Proficient in identifying, evaluating, and prioritizing risks.
 Attention to Detail – Precise in identifying errors and discrepancies.
 Communication Skills – Able to communicate clearly through reports and presentations.
 Problem-Solving Skills – Capable to identify issues and propose solutions.
 Teamwork oriented – Promoting collaboration and teamwork.
 Excellent oral and written communications skills in French and English
 
 
 Others Swiss Residence 
 Travel might be expected at up to 20% 
 
 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

 Attention to Detail – Precise in identifying errors and discrepancies.
 Communication Skills – Able to communicate clearly through reports and presentations.
 Problem-Solving Skills – Capable to identify issues and propose solutions.
 Teamwork oriented – Promoting collaboration and teamwork.
 Excellent oral and written communications skills in French and English
 
 
 Others Swiss Residence 
 Travel might be expected at up to 20% 
 
 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Graduate Programme Jersey, 2026, Wealth Management
jobs.description:
Job Description - Please note we do not offer sponsorship
 
This is a unique 12-month programme based on a permanent contract within our Wealth Management Team in Jersey. We are offering an exceptional opportunity for a recent master’s graduate to join our exclusive programme, designed to provide in-depth exposure to Wealth Management at a renowned Private Bank.
The programme offers a comprehensive learning experience, enabling you to gain valuable insights into the finance industry and develop essential skills for a successful career in private banking.
 
The graduate programme consists of 3 rotations each lasting 4 months. These rotations take place in different teams, all closely connected to Wealth Management and Treasury and Trading, providing an excellent opportunity to build a broad internal network of colleagues and experts. Furthermore, you will benefit from shadowing experiences, gaining valuable exposure to various aspects of the bank’s operation.
This programme is specifically designed for young graduates holding a master’s degree, with a maximum of 12 months of professional experience since graduation. You will have the opportunity to transition into a permanent role within the team after completing the programme.
 
Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) – Wealth Management:
Union Bancaire Privée (UBP) is one of the largest family-owned private banks in the world. But what makes UBP unique is its corporate culture, which combines modernity and tradition. Although UBP was founded in 1969, its origins can be traced back to the 17th century and the start of a long line of financiers and diplomats of whom Edgar de Picciotto and his descendants are the successors. Today, the Bank's staff draw their inspiration and drive from the founding family's subtle blend of strong moral values and pragmatism, which to this day remain the key to UBP’s success.
 
As it plays such a major part in our strategy, we are investing significantly in Wealth Management to fuel our growth, particularly in Europe, the Middle East, Eastern Europe, Latin America, and Asia. We provide a complete range of private banking products and services, including a variety of investment management and advisory mandates, tailored to our clients’ specific requirements, objectives, and risk profiles. Among UBP professional partners, over 300 independent asset managers have chosen us as their custodian bank, valuing our personalised, flexible services and our transparent fee structure.
 
 Role Description:
 
You will be part of the Wealth Management Team in Jersey:
Relationship with private clients and marketing
With the guidance of relationship managers and experts:

Cultivate relationships with clients

Respond to clients’/prospects’ needs with investments proposals

Ensure that contractual documents and supporting paperwork are signed and up to date

Comply with the Bank’s internal directives, considering the client’s profile and types of transactions
Assisting relationship managers With the guidance of relationship managers and experts:

Follow up on and execute administrative tasks: account paperwork filing, transaction executions, trade executions, transfers, discretionary management and advisory mandate processing, client query responses

Ensure clients’ transactions/instructions/requests (stock market orders, transfers, tax report requests, product information, etc.) are executed and addressed correctly and swiftly

Provide administrative support to the relationship managers by preparing visits and trips, filing client data and documents, and checking that clients’ investments are consistent with their risk profiles
UNION BANCAIRE PRIVÉE, UBP SA | WM GRADUATE PROGRAMME | MARCH26 2 | 3

Carry out first-level checks as per internal directives, where applicable, and escalate transactions or situations putting the Bank at risk, to the line manager
Treasury and Trading
With the guidance of the Treasury and Trading Manager

Forecast near and long-term cash follows across the entity

Maintain buffers and contingency liquidity

Coordinate intercompany funding and optimise notional/physical pooling
Funding and Capital Markets
With the guidance of the Treasury and Trading Manager

Plan and execute short- and long-term funding, i.e. bonds and loans

Maintain bank and investor relationships

Monitor costs of funds and refinance risk, manage maturities
Risk Management
With the guidance of the Treasury and Trading Manager

Identify exposures

Set and enforce risk limits, hedging and effective metrics

Execute hedges via forward, swaps and options

Stress testing, sensitivity analysis and scenario planning
Compliance with regulations and internal directives

Learn to know and strictly apply all internal directives and rules, baking laws and regulations, such as those regarding entering business relations with clients, contacts/relations with clients (existing or prospective), anti-money-laundering, cross-border business, investment policies, investor protection, business travel

Attend the Bank's compulsory training session
Requirements:

Educational Background: Master’s degree in financial economics recently obtained from a highly recognised university

Work Experience: Relevant initial work experience in the finance industry (internships), preferably within Private Banking or Wealth Management

Technical Skills: Strong knowledge of Excel

Language Proficiency: Fluency in English is required

Key Skills: Strong analytical skills, structured approach, excellent communication, and a proactive, adaptable mindset. Ability to collaborate across business and technical teams, with a self-motivated, results-driven attitude

Work Authorisation: Applicants must have the legal right to work in the UK and Jersey at the time of application. We do not offer sponsorship

Jersey residential and employment status
Applications Process: We only accept online applications submitted through our official website, ensure that your application includes the following documents:

CV

Motivation Letter

Grade Transcript & Diplomas

Reference Letters
UNION BANCAIRE PRIVÉE, UBP SA | WM GRADUATE PROGRAMME | MARCH26 3 | 3
Key Dates: ¦ Programme start date: 1st September 2026 ¦ Duration of programme: 12 months (permanent contract) ¦ Interviews Period: March to June 2026

jobs.title: Graduate Programme - Investment Services
jobs.description:
Permanent position with a 12-months rotational program within different Investment Services teams to discover different activities, as well as build expertise and professional skills. Rotation will be arranged for the graduate within IS organization including but not limited to 
 
 - Fixed Income / Equity / Funds / Private Markets research and Advisory 
 
 - Investment Advisory and Discretionary Portfolio Management Services 
 
 - Product Governance and management. 
 

 During the program, under supervision of various Team Leaders / Heads as mentor , the Graduate is expected to gain exposure to the following functional areas: 
 
 - Private banking investment advisory framework 
 
 - Discretionary Portfolio Management Investment Process and portfolio construction. 
 
 - Equity / Fixed Income / external fund manager selection process 
 
 - Building blocks of investment portfolios 
 
 - Product risk classification and client suitability frameworks for private banks 

 At the end of all rotations, the Graduate is expected to be trained in: 
 
 - Asset class knowledge - gain some understanding and application of the basic process of screening investment products for opportunities. 
 
 - Portfolio Management and Construction - gain some understanding and application of the basic process of portfolio management and construction 
 
 - Market profiling – support the development of marketing pitches, as well as propositions for new product. 
 
 - Market Research and Analysis – continuously develop investment acumen in the context of investing for high-net-worth individuals and entities. 
 
 - Product Management - gain some understanding of the Product control landscape integral to the product governance framework. 

 Performing regulated activities 
 
 -As part of the integration of the Graduate into the Investment Services team and his/her development to eventually specialise in at least one asset class or service, he/she is expected to take part in regulated activities conducted by the specialists, advisors and Portfolio managers across asset classes. 
 
 -These activities will be conducted under the supervision of the respective team heads, and include but are not limited to: 
 
 - Assisting RMs and Advisors to explain particular investment propositions in key client pitches and meetings. 
 
 - Arranging of timely and regular updates of key instruments held by clients to RMs as part of the comprehensive 
 
 “after- 
 sales” advisory service. 
 
 - Assisting in the development and production of investment proposals and presentations to clients and prospects 
 
 - Working with Portfolio managers on portfolio construction and re-balancing. 
 
 - Assisting asset class specialist in their activities pertain to research as well as preparation of research / 
 
 
 marketing 
 materials. 
 
 - Contribute to strategic and tactical trading ideas suitable for the bank’s client base. 

 Other expected tasks 
 
 - Assist product specialists monitoring investment product exposure to provide necessary performance update to clients and front office teams 
 
 - Perform market analysis across coverage sectors and companies in the UBP product universe and provide research communication to front office teams. 
 
 - Coordinate with business management, compliance, middle office team, and other key stakeholders to ensure investment 
 product offerings, discretionary and advisory services are in compliant with UBP policy. 
 
 - Monitor client portfolios to update front office teams for any house view changes, performance alert, investment opportunities, portfolio rebalancing needs, and etc. 
 
 - Participate implementation of investment campaign and global launches providing necessary marketing supports to front office teams including product training, marketing materials, order taking, client meetings, joint calls, and etc. 

 Rationale – Why Investment Services? 
 We are a Singapore-based team comprising of various investment teams on the Wealth Management platform that offers Clients the access to UBP’s institutional expertise. Our mission consists of leveraging the UBP local and global investment expertise to propose best in class and timely conviction-driven investment opportunities. Combining Wealth Management Specialist, discretionary and Advisory expertise, our integrated team brings consistently prudent analysis and conviction-driven, in-depth guidance to our clients. Our thought leadership is channelled through our internal and Client-friendly materials including House View, Advisory/Market updates, Macro outlook and Asset Allocation pitches. 
 Candidates may consider applying if they are eager to contribute and develop a career as an Investment specialist (multi-asset or an individual asset class / service) who will eventually be responsible to offer investment advice, help manage portfolios and / or handle technical areas, such as the evaluation, development and implementation of Investment product strategy for our Clients and the UBP organisation. 
 Skills 
 Basic knowledge in excel VBA / at least a programming language e.g. Javascript, Python and C++, will be a plus 

 Proficiency in Mandarin would be a plus

 -As part of the integration of the Graduate into the Investment Services team and his/her development to eventually specialise in at least one asset class or service, he/she is expected to take part in regulated activities conducted by the specialists, advisors and Portfolio managers across asset classes. 
 
 -These activities will be conducted under the supervision of the respective team heads, and include but are not limited to: 
 
 - Assisting RMs and Advisors to explain particular investment propositions in key client pitches and meetings. 
 
 - Arranging of timely and regular updates of key instruments held by clients to RMs as part of the comprehensive 
 
 “after- 
 sales” advisory service. 
 
 - Assisting in the development and production of investment proposals and presentations to clients and prospects 
 
 - Working with Portfolio managers on portfolio construction and re-balancing. 
 
 - Assisting asset class specialist in their activities pertain to research as well as preparation of research / 
 
 
 marketing 
 materials. 
 
 - Contribute to strategic and tactical trading ideas suitable for the bank’s client base. 

 Other expected tasks 
 
 - Assist product specialists monitoring investment product exposure to provide necessary performance update to clients and front office teams 
 
 - Perform market analysis across coverage sectors and companies in the UBP product universe and provide research communication to front office teams. 
 
 - Coordinate with business management, compliance, middle office team, and other key stakeholders to ensure investment 
 product offerings, discretionary and advisory services are in compliant with UBP policy. 
 
 - Monitor client portfolios to update front office teams for any house view changes, performance alert, investment opportunities, portfolio rebalancing needs, and etc. 
 
 - Participate implementation of investment campaign and global launches providing necessary marketing supports to front office teams including product training, marketing materials, order taking, client meetings, joint calls, and etc. 

 Rationale – Why Investment Services? 
 We are a Singapore-based team comprising of various investment teams on the Wealth Management platform that offers Clients the access to UBP’s institutional expertise. Our mission consists of leveraging the UBP local and global investment expertise to propose best in class and timely conviction-driven investment opportunities. Combining Wealth Management Specialist, discretionary and Advisory expertise, our integrated team brings consistently prudent analysis and conviction-driven, in-depth guidance to our clients. Our thought leadership is channelled through our internal and Client-friendly materials including House View, Advisory/Market updates, Macro outlook and Asset Allocation pitches. 
 Candidates may consider applying if they are eager to contribute and develop a career as an Investment specialist (multi-asset or an individual asset class / service) who will eventually be responsible to offer investment advice, help manage portfolios and / or handle technical areas, such as the evaluation, development and implementation of Investment product strategy for our Clients and the UBP organisation. 
 Skills 
 Basic knowledge in excel VBA / at least a programming language e.g. Javascript, Python and C++, will be a plus 

 Proficiency in Mandarin would be a plus

jobs.title: EAM Account Manager (6 months contract)
jobs.description:
Mission 
 The Account Manager delivers and promotes a culture of Quality, Respect and Excellence to External Asset Managers and their end-clients, to Relationship Managers responsible for the EAM relationships as well as to various internal teams and departments. 
 Main responsibilities 
 Process and manage all operational requests received from Relationship Managers and External Asset Managers 
 Coordinate across operational teams to ensure smooth execution of activities and timely follow-up 
 Provide high-quality support and proactively propose solutions and improvements on assigned topics 
 Ensure accurate and swift execution of operational transactions, instructions and requests from RMs and EAMs 
 Take ownership, input and follow-up on Securities and Structured Products orders, Fund orders, Private Equity, Limited Partnerships, Forex and Swap orders, payment orders, cheques and standing orders, Securities and Fund transfers, Credit cards and Fiduciary deposits and loans 
 Handle administrative requests (investigations, account and tax statements as well as specific ad-hoc requests) 
 
 

 Your Profile 
 Degree in a related field (banking apprenticeship, CYP, banking internship or similar) 
 Private Banking certificate, CWMA or other equivalent certification a plus 
 Minimum 3 years of experience in a similar role in a Private Bank or External Asset Managers 
 Strong banking and KYC knowledge 
 Strong team spirit, inter-personal skills, open-minded, highly service and client oriented as well as excellent communication skills 
 Fluent in French and English. Any additional language would be a strong added value 
 
 Others 
 Swiss Residence 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Graduate programme, London, Marketing & COO 2026
jobs.description:
Job Description 
 (Please do not apply if you require a visa). We do not offer sponsorship. You will need a British passport/ Dual passport or ILR - Indefine leave to remain). 
 
 This unique 12-month graduate programme is based in London and offers a permanent contract. The exceptional opportunity for a recent master’s graduate is designed to provide in-depth, practical exposure to various areas of the business within a renowned Private Bank. Created as a comprehensive learning experience, enabling you to gain valuable insights into the finance industry and develop essential skills for a successful career in private banking. 
 
 The programme is structured around two rotations of 6 months – in the COO (Chief Operating Officer) team and Communications and Marketing team – after which the candidate will join the bank on a permanent basis. 
 This programme is specifically focused on young graduates holding a master’s degree, with a maximum of 12 months of professional experience since graduation. Upon successful completion, you will have the opportunity to transition into a permanent role within one of the teams, continuing your career with the bank and applying the knowledge, networks, and experience gained during the programme in a role aligned to your strengths and interests. 
 
 Role Description: 
 The role will have a broad remit: 
 As part of the Communications team, the successful candidate will gain exposure to various initiatives designed to communicate effectively with internal and external stakeholders and promoting the brand. The candidate will report to Head of Communications and Marketing and will collaborate with the wider Communications team in the UK and in Geneva / other global locations. 
 The key areas of focus will include: Preparing external and internal communications 
 Drafting briefing notes for spokespeople for media engagements and events 
 Supporting internal and client events 
 Supporting delivery of digital campaigns 
 

 
 In the COO team the successful candidate will report to the Head of COO Office Strategy and will get exposure to COO teams: Operations, Technology, HR, Legal, Change Management and Strategy. You may work on topics such as: Building dashboards, KPIs, budgeting 
 Analyse processes and suggest improvements 
 Support delivery of strategic initiatives for example by tracking milestones, risks, dependencies 
 Support data quality initiatives 
 Prepare materials and packs for forums or operational committees 
 

 
 Requirements: 
 Educational Background: Recent graduate ( Master’s degree) in Finance, Business, Marketing, Media and PR, Economics or Engineering from a highly recognised university. 
 Work Experience : Relevant initial work experience in the finance industry (internship) is an advantage but not mandatory. 
 Technical Skills: Strong knowledge of MS Office tools – Excel, Word, Power Point. 
 Language Proficiency: Fluency in English is required. 
 Key Skills: 
 Excellent verbal and written communication skills 
 Ability to engage and collaborate with people from different business areas 
 Strong analytical and data skills- comfortable working with data and numbers 
 Strong problem-solving skills, results-driven attitude, proactive mindset 
 Structured approach 
 
 Work Authorization: Applicants must have the legal right to work in the UK at the time of application. We do not offer sponsorship. 
 
 **** 
 Applications Process: 
 We only accept online applications submitted through our official website. 
 Ensure that your application includes the following documents: 
 -CV 
 -Motivation Letter 
 -Grade Transcript & Diplomas 
 -Reference Letters 
 Key Dates: 
 ■ Programme start date: 1st September 2026 
 ■ Duration of programme: 12 months (permanent contract) 
 ■ Interviews Period: March to June 2026

 The programme is structured around two rotations of 6 months – in the COO (Chief Operating Officer) team and Communications and Marketing team – after which the candidate will join the bank on a permanent basis. 
 This programme is specifically focused on young graduates holding a master’s degree, with a maximum of 12 months of professional experience since graduation. Upon successful completion, you will have the opportunity to transition into a permanent role within one of the teams, continuing your career with the bank and applying the knowledge, networks, and experience gained during the programme in a role aligned to your strengths and interests. 
 
 Role Description: 
 The role will have a broad remit: 
 As part of the Communications team, the successful candidate will gain exposure to various initiatives designed to communicate effectively with internal and external stakeholders and promoting the brand. The candidate will report to Head of Communications and Marketing and will collaborate with the wider Communications team in the UK and in Geneva / other global locations. 
 The key areas of focus will include: Preparing external and internal communications 
 Drafting briefing notes for spokespeople for media engagements and events 
 Supporting internal and client events 
 Supporting delivery of digital campaigns 
 

 
 In the COO team the successful candidate will report to the Head of COO Office Strategy and will get exposure to COO teams: Operations, Technology, HR, Legal, Change Management and Strategy. You may work on topics such as: Building dashboards, KPIs, budgeting 
 Analyse processes and suggest improvements 
 Support delivery of strategic initiatives for example by tracking milestones, risks, dependencies 
 Support data quality initiatives 
 Prepare materials and packs for forums or operational committees 
 

 
 Requirements: 
 Educational Background: Recent graduate ( Master’s degree) in Finance, Business, Marketing, Media and PR, Economics or Engineering from a highly recognised university. 
 Work Experience : Relevant initial work experience in the finance industry (internship) is an advantage but not mandatory. 
 Technical Skills: Strong knowledge of MS Office tools – Excel, Word, Power Point. 
 Language Proficiency: Fluency in English is required. 
 Key Skills: 
 Excellent verbal and written communication skills 
 Ability to engage and collaborate with people from different business areas 
 Strong analytical and data skills- comfortable working with data and numbers 
 Strong problem-solving skills, results-driven attitude, proactive mindset 
 Structured approach 
 
 Work Authorization: Applicants must have the legal right to work in the UK at the time of application. We do not offer sponsorship. 
 
 **** 
 Applications Process: 
 We only accept online applications submitted through our official website. 
 Ensure that your application includes the following documents: 
 -CV 
 -Motivation Letter 
 -Grade Transcript & Diplomas 
 -Reference Letters 
 Key Dates: 
 ■ Programme start date: 1st September 2026 
 ■ Duration of programme: 12 months (permanent contract) 
 ■ Interviews Period: March to June 2026

jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: Junior HR Assistant/Coordinator (5 months)
jobs.description:
Du möchtest erste Erfahrung im HR sammeln und bringst eine abgeschlossene Lehre, idealerweise mit HR-Weiterbildung mit? Ich suche eine/n Junior HR Assistant/Coordinator, der/die mich im Daily HR Business unterstützt. Du übernimmst Terminierung und Kandidatenkommunikation, und unterstützt unser HR-Team in Zürich bei administrativen und operativen Aufgaben. Die Stelle ist temporär für 5 Monate, 100% und bietet dir die ideale Möglichkeit, wertvolle Einblicke in die HR-Praxis einer Privatbank zu gewinnen. 
 
 Recruiting und Interview-Unterstützung 
 Terminierung von Interviews 

 Versand von Einladungen 

 Abstimmung mit Hiring Managern und Kandidaten 

 Organisation von Interviews (virtuell und vor Ort) 

 Pflege von Interviewkalendern sowie Kandidaten-Kommunikation vor und nach Interviews 

 Unterstützung beim Screening (Vorselektion) 

 
 Referenzen und Background-Checks 
 Einholung und Dokumentation von Referenzen 

 Koordination und Verwaltung notwendiger Unterlagen 

 
 Dokumentenmanagement und Archivierung 
 Scannen, benennen, kategorisieren und revisionssichere Ablage von Personalunterlagen gemäss Datenschutzvorgaben 

 
 Operative Unterstützung im HR-Tagesgeschäft 
 Unterstützung entlang des gesamten Employee Lifecycles; Eintritt, Austritt, etc.) 

 Payroll-Unterstützung 

 Postverkehr 

 
 Event-Unterstützung 
 Planung und Unterstützung von HR-Events, Onboarding Days, Recruiting-Events, etc. 

 Einladungsmanagement, Logistik, Teilnehmerlisten, etc. 

 
 Administrative Aufgaben 
 Pflege von HR-Systemen 

 Erstellung einfacher Auswertungen/Reports, etc. 

 Pflege von Übersichten, Aktualisierung von Checklisten, etc. 

 
 
 
 Qualifikationen: 
 Abgeschlossene kaufmännische Berufslehre (E- oder M-Profil), idealerweise mit HR-Schwerpunkt 

 Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse (mindestens C1) 

 Ausgeprägte Detailorientierung und sorgfältige Arbeitsweise 

 Hohe Teamfähigkeit und Serviceorientierung 

 Diskreter Umgang mit sensiblen Daten und Compliance-Bewusstsein 

 Von Vorteil: HR-Weiterbilung / HR-Assistant 

 Von Vorteil: Erfahrung im Banking- oder Finanumfeld (Bankelehre) 

 
 Start: sofort 
 Ort: UBP Zürich 
 Für diese Position ist Home Office leider nicht möglich
