jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: Accounts Payable Officer
jobs.description:
Mission 
 The Invoice Officer in Accounts Payable is responsible for the accurate processing of invoices and ensuring timely payments to vendors (approximately 350 invoices per month). 
 This role involves maintaining financial records, resolving discrepancies, and supporting the overall account payable function. 
 The Invoice Officer will also collaborate closely with IT procurement and business IT partners to enhance operational efficiency and ensure alignment with financial practices. 
 
 Main responsibilities include but not limited to : 
 
 1. Invoice Processing: 
 - Review and verify invoices for accuracy and completeness. 
 - Enter invoices into the information system (Coupa/Dynamics Navision) and ensure proper coding. 
 - Participate in projects focused on processes transformation 
 
 2. Payment Processing: 
 - Prepare and process payment runs. 
 - Monitor payment schedules and ensure timely payments to vendors. 
 
 3. Vendor Management: 
 - Communicate with vendors regarding invoice status and payment inquiries. 
 - Assist in resolving any disputes or discrepancies. 
 
 4. Record Keeping: 
 - Maintain accurate and organized accounts payable records. 
 - Prepare and manage monthly accruals to ensure accurate financial reporting and compliance with accounting standards. 
 - Provide detailed business allocation of depreciation for projects 
 - Ensure all invoices are filed according to company policy. 
 
 5. Reporting: 
 - Assist in generating accounts payable reports for management review. 
 - Support month-end closing activities related to accounts payable. 
 - Provide detailed report related to mobile communication costs on a quarterly basis. 
 
 6. Compliance: 
 - Ensure compliance with company policies and accounting standards. 
 - Assist in audits by providing necessary documentation and information. 
 
 Qualifications: 
 

 Associate’s or Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field preferred. 
 Previous experience in accounts payable or a similar role is mandatory. 
 Previous experience with Coupa and MS Dynamics is preferable 
 Proficient in accounting software and Microsoft Office Suite. 
 Excellent attention to detail and accuracy in data entry and financial reporting. 
 Proficient verbal and written communication skills in both French and English for effective collaboration with vendors and internal teams. 
 Ability to work independently and as part of a team. 
 
 Others Swiss Residence 
 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Process Transformation Officer
jobs.description:
Mission: 
 Support the continuous improvement of Financial Crime Compliance (FCC) processes (AML, KYC, Sanctions) by working closely with Compliance, Business, and IT teams. Contribute to aligning Compliance requirements with operational processes and IT tools while enhancing process efficiency and establishing a robust control framework. 
 Responsibilities 
 Support the analysis of business needs and contribute to integrating compliance requirements into financial crime prevention processes (AML, KYC, Sanctions). Assist in identifying process improvement opportunities and implementing solutions validated by stakeholders (Compliance, Front Office, IT, etc.). 

 Design and implement streamlined, scalable, and effective processes to enhance operational performance and compliance with regulatory requirements. 

 Participate in the documentation and updating of processes related to compliance, including Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), ensuring alignment with current practices and regulatory requirements. 

 Collaborate with cross-functional teams (e.g. Tools & Projects teams, etc.) for the development, maintenance, and testing of compliance applications (IMTF, smartKYC, Pythagoras, etc.). 

 Contribute to change management , particularly by supporting training and communication initiatives for users. 

 Participate in functional testing of implemented solutions, using provided methodologies and tools, and ensuring rigorous follow-up of results. 

 Support the improvement of control frameworks related to compliance tools, including monitoring incidents, analyzing data quality, and collecting user feedback. 

 Assist in monitoring compliance tools , ensuring the proper functioning of data flows, updating parameters, and conducting regular review exercises in collaboration with IT and Tools & Projects teams. 

 Contribute to project management related to AML, KYC, and sanctions by participating in local or group initiatives and engaging in project management methodologies (Agile, Waterfall). 

 Participate in technological watch and identify opportunities to improve compliance tools and processes. 

 Support the follow-up of audit recommendations related to operational compliance processes, ensuring their implementation within the required deadlines. 

 
 Your Profil 
 Education and experience: experience in compliance financial crime, risk management, or operational processes is preferred. At least 5 years of experience and experience in a consulting firm (Big 4 ou equivalent) is an asset 

 Analytical skills: Ability to analyze processes, identify ineficciencies, gaps and areas for improvement, and propose appropriate solutions. 

 Communication skills: Proficiency in drafting clear and structured documents, as well as communicating effectively with various stakeholders. 

 Team spirit: Ability to collaborate effectively with multidisciplinary teams (Compliance, Business, IT) and adapt to different interlocutors. 

 Organization and rigor: Ability to manage multiple tasks and projects simultaneously while respecting deadlines and priorities. 

 Technical knowledge: familiarity with compliance technology solutions, data analytics tools, and automation platforms. Experience or interest in data management, reporting, or process modeling tools is a plus. 

 Languages: Proficiency in French and English, both written and spoken. 

 Swiss residence: Mandatory 

 Core competencies 
 Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility, and Responsibility. 

 Compliance with regulations and internal directives 

 Curiosity and willingness to learn in a constantly evolving environment. 

 Why Join Our Team? 
 You will join a dynamic and collaborative team where you will have the opportunity to develop your skills in compliance and project management while benefiting from guidance and support to advance your career. You will be involved in strategic and innovative projects at the heart of FCC department. 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Phone Operator
jobs.description:
Mission Manage a high-volume, high-standard French/English switchboard (additional languages are an asset), administer the VOIP system (AGILE), and handle CFF travel arrangements.

 Join a private bank and interact with a wide range of stakeholders: various departments, VIPs, and senior executives.

 
 Main responsibilities 
 
 CISCO VOIP system management: 
 
 Handle external and internal calls with strict confidentiality and exceptional courtesy.

 Rigorously enforce security standards, particularly regarding phishing attempts and malicious calls.

 Manage call transfers and message taking using the Samwin operator console. 

 Cover Executive Management calls during assistants’ breaks and absences, as well as for certain management groups.

 
 CFF travel management: 
 Provide support and advice for domestic CFF travel. 

 Process requests for international travel. 

 Manage the renewal of CFF subscriptions.

 
 Ticketing and facilities requests: 
 Administer the ticketing system for facility management requests (e.g., ventilation). 

 Coordinate with relevant stakeholders, ensure follow-up, and close action items.

 
 

 Your Profile 
 Experience: 
 
 At least 5 years of experience in a similar role within a highly demanding environment is required Technical skills 
 

 Languages: French and English must be mastered perfectly (minimum C1 level). 
 Any additional language is an asset. 
 
 Personal skills: Motivated, dynamic, proactive, and compassionate professional with a service mindset. 
 Natural aptitude for pedagogy, able to make technical topics accessible and easy to understand. 
 Active listening skills with both internal and external stakeholders. 
 Strong stress management, maintaining composure in all circumstances. 
 Discreet and tactful, exercising restraint and professionalism at all times. 
 Team-oriented, committed to sharing knowledge, fostering collective intelligence, and learning from the surrounding experience. 
 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: Funds Execution Officer
jobs.description:
Mission 
 Ensure the accurate and timely execution of client fund orders across Mutual Funds, Hedge Funds, and Private Equity Funds for all UBP branches, while maintaining strict compliance with internal processes and regulatory requirements. Deliver exceptional service to clients to ensure seamless end-to-end fund order processing.
 Main responsibilities 
 
 Execute client fund orders across Mutual Funds, Hedge Funds, Private Equity. Mutual funds are processed primarily via SWIFT through a global custodian while Alternatives (HF/PE) are placed directly with fund administrators, including completion of required subscription/redemption documentation and KYC/AML. Coordinate funding, settlement, and confirmations; manage pre-funding, notice periods, and specific terms (gates/lock-ups).
 Your Profile 
 
 Education and Technical Skills 
 Bachelor’s degree in Economics (or equivalent). 
 Proficient in Microsoft Office tools (Excel, Word, Outlook, VBA a plus). 
 Familiarity with fund order workflows and transfer agent processes environments is an asset
 

 Personal Skills 
 Think in “Problem–Action–Results” terms; proactive and driven. 
 Strong client service ethic; clear and professional communicator. 
 Highly organized with excellent attention to detail; able to manage tight deadlines and work under pressure in a high-volume setting. 
 Strong analytical skills with the ability to identify issues, propose solutions, and follow through to completion. 
 Constructive, collaborative, and committed to continuous learning. 
 Adaptable, self-motivated, and a team player. 
 Sound understanding of operational risks in fund execution with disciplined control and timely escalation. 
 
 Languages 
 French and English: fluent (written and spoken).
 
 Others Swiss Residence 
 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

 Adaptable, self-motivated, and a team player. 
 Sound understanding of operational risks in fund execution with disciplined control and timely escalation. 
 
 Languages 
 French and English: fluent (written and spoken).
 
 Others Swiss Residence 
 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.source: Union Bancaire Privée Oracle HCM API

jobs.title: Quantitative & Risk Analyst
jobs.description:
Mission 
 Build, enhance, and maintain quantitative processes and applications to support: 
 Instrument risk modelling 
 Portfolio and investment risk management 
 Financial screening and investment proposal solutions 
 Data engineering and database management 
 Quantitative reporting and analytics
 
 Main responsibilities 
 You will join a 10-person Risk and Quantitative Analysis team serving both Asset Management and Wealth Management. You’ll leverage strong coding and financial skills to contribute to core functions across risk modeling, portfolio risk oversight, and quantitative infrastructure such as: 
 Investment Risk Management Develop, calibrate, and automate risk models for client portfolio instruments, including derivatives and structured products.
 Oversee portfolio risk across strategies and mandates; produce risk metrics, ensure daily data quality checks, manage exceptions.
 Recommend portfolio adjustments based on risk optimization, style analysis, and stress scenarios.
 Partner with portfolio managers to translate risk insights into actionable proposals.
 
 Quant Infrastructure and Data Maintain and extend the team’s data pipelines and tooling; collect, compute, and process market, reference, and portfolio data. Enforce data lineage, versioning, and audit trails.
 Participate to the maintenance and development of instrument screening tools, across various asset classes.
 Contribute to quantitative reporting for external and internal stakeholders.
 
 Investment Proposal Tool Participate to the maintenance and development of an investment proposal tool, for both discretionary mandates and institutional clients.
 
 
 Your Profile 
 Minimum 3-year experience in a similar role or equivalent
 Degree in Quantitative Economics, Mathematics, IT, Finance, or equivalent. 
 Strong IT skills: Python, C#, SQL, VBA. Experience in developing APIs a plus.
 Experience with external tools such as BQuant or APT a plus
 Analytical mind with ability to explain technical subjects in a fluent way
 Client orientated with an excellent service delivery
 Team oriented with the capacity to work autonomously while reporting to the direct supervisor
 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Investor Protection
jobs.description:
Main Responsibilities 
 The UK Regulatory Compliance Team is primarily responsible for providing timely, risk-based advice, challenge, and enablement to the business on regulatory matters. The Investor Protection Compliance team sits within UK Regulatory Compliance and assists in managing compliance risk across UBP UK, contributing to regulatory change implementation, and helping to maintain effective policies, training, and governance in line with UK requirements and UBP standards relating to investor and client protection. Investor Protection Compliance ensure investors and clients are treated fairly and transparently, topics include but are not limited to; disclosures, suitability & appropriateness, conflicts of interest, complaints handling, marketing and client communications, vulnerable customers, responsible lending, product governance and best execution. 
 
 The role holder is responsible for: 
 supporting the team in fulfilling the responsibilities set out above. 

 supporting the team and assisting in the management of compliance risk across UBP UK’s business. In providing advisory support, the role holder will liaise with colleagues in the UK Regulatory Compliance team, Financial Crime and overseas offices as appropriate to ensure local regulations and wider department processes are considered. 

 providing advice and enabling support and independent challenge to any areas of the UBP business or any topics as agreed with management. The role holder will also be expected to support business projects and provide cover for other members of the team as required. 

 coordinating team activities, including but not limited to managing the mailbox and query management, new product and service reviews, product and service ongoing reviews and financial promotions. 

 reviewing financial promotions, marketing materials and client facing communications. 

 initial reviews and coordinating the update of relevant policies and processes considering UK regulatory requirements and market and business development 

 assisting with the analysis of regulatory issues arising and implementation of relevant projects and regulatory changes 

 working with and supporting the team, to respond to advisory queries, upgrade and implement policies considering regulatory developments, and contribute to risk assessments, training and internal governance reporting activity as required. 

 
 Role Duties 
 Investor Protection Compliance support and coordination: 
 Assist in monitoring external regulatory and business developments and help develop and implement policies and procedures to reflect new requirements 

 Support in analysis of regulatory developments where required for investor protection topics 

 Assist in analysing and preparing for the impact of upcoming regulatory developments 

 Contribute to compliance reporting, dashboards, and management updates, ensuring data is timely, accurate, and consistent. 

 Contribute to improving the process and procedures to meet consumer duty principles and other conduct requirements within the regulatory framework, Treating Customers Fairly and sound risk management 

 Provide stakeholders with sound and timely advice, guidance and support, where appropriate. 

 Assist in the Regular review of Investor Protection related Policies and other written guidance. 

 Assist in evaluating and progressing proposed new products/ services and other business initiatives. 

 Assist in developing and maintaining training, communications, and guidance that translates regulatory expectations into practical steps for the business. 

 Initial review and investigation, where required, of escalations from business assurance relating to; cross border, client-opt ups, suitability and appropriateness. 

 Review of regular reports related to complaints from business assurance 

 Help to identify regulatory compliance risks. 

 Team Management and Collaboration: 
 Uphold the UBP values and, by example, support culture of compliance with policies, appropriate risk management and the principle of treating customers fairly. 

 Support management and the team through active participation in regular team and 1-2-1 meetings 

 Partner with business stakeholders to support compliance objectives and business enablement 

 Participate in group projects as appropriate and required 

 Work within the team to help identify areas for improvement and efficiency 

 Support the Compliance team and act as cover for other members where required. 

 Business & Market Knowledge 
 Develop and sustain technical knowledge across the products and services of UBP to ensure business is supported. 

 Retain updated personal knowledge of wealth management/private banking market, ensuring that the reputation of UBP is upheld to the highest level possible in terms of compliance and reputation internally and externally and to consider treating customers fairly and good customer outcomes. 

 Internal Control 
 Maintain full, accurate files/notes of key meetings and advice using UBP core systems to store. 

 Help log, triage, and track compliance issues, incidents, and inquiries 

 Support root-cause analysis and follow-up actions under guidance from senior team members. 

 The job description is not exhaustive and may include other duties as assigned from time to time by management. Specific duties may evolve based on business needs, regulatory changes, and the candidate’s strengths and interests. 
 
 Committee Membership 
 None 
 
 Conduct Rules 
 This role is required to comply with the Financial Conduct Authority’s (FCA) Individual Conduct Rules, which are: 
 
 To act with integrity; 
 To act with due skill, care and diligence; 
 To be open and cooperative with the FCA, the Prudential Regulation Authoritity (PRA) and other regulators; 
 To pay due regard to the interests of customers and treat them fairly; 
 To observe proper standards of market conduct; and 
 To act to deliver good outcomes for retail customers. 
 
 Person Specification 
 Knowledge, Skills & Experience 
 
 Good understanding of UK financial regulations including but not limited to: MiFID II, Consumer Duty, FCA rulebook 

 Compliance qualifications (ICA or CISI) desirable 

 2 years+ Financial Services experience preferable 

 Wealth management experience is desirable 

 Ability to assess and analyse information 

 Ability to interpret regulation, identify risks and analyse potential control gaps 

 Strong attention to detail and focus 

 Good written & communication skills 

 Numeracy, drafting and record keeping 

 Team Spirit & Collaboration 

 Attention to detail and focus 

 Good project / change management skills

 Partner with business stakeholders to support compliance objectives and business enablement 

 Participate in group projects as appropriate and required 

 Work within the team to help identify areas for improvement and efficiency 

 Support the Compliance team and act as cover for other members where required. 

 Business & Market Knowledge 
 Develop and sustain technical knowledge across the products and services of UBP to ensure business is supported. 

 Retain updated personal knowledge of wealth management/private banking market, ensuring that the reputation of UBP is upheld to the highest level possible in terms of compliance and reputation internally and externally and to consider treating customers fairly and good customer outcomes. 

 Internal Control 
 Maintain full, accurate files/notes of key meetings and advice using UBP core systems to store. 

 Help log, triage, and track compliance issues, incidents, and inquiries 

 Support root-cause analysis and follow-up actions under guidance from senior team members. 

 The job description is not exhaustive and may include other duties as assigned from time to time by management. Specific duties may evolve based on business needs, regulatory changes, and the candidate’s strengths and interests. 
 
 Committee Membership 
 None 
 
 Conduct Rules 
 This role is required to comply with the Financial Conduct Authority’s (FCA) Individual Conduct Rules, which are: 
 
 To act with integrity; 
 To act with due skill, care and diligence; 
 To be open and cooperative with the FCA, the Prudential Regulation Authoritity (PRA) and other regulators; 
 To pay due regard to the interests of customers and treat them fairly; 
 To observe proper standards of market conduct; and 
 To act to deliver good outcomes for retail customers. 
 
 Person Specification 
 Knowledge, Skills & Experience 
 
 Good understanding of UK financial regulations including but not limited to: MiFID II, Consumer Duty, FCA rulebook 

 Compliance qualifications (ICA or CISI) desirable 

 2 years+ Financial Services experience preferable 

 Wealth management experience is desirable 

 Ability to assess and analyse information 

 Ability to interpret regulation, identify risks and analyse potential control gaps 

 Strong attention to detail and focus 

 Good written & communication skills 

 Numeracy, drafting and record keeping 

 Team Spirit & Collaboration 

 Attention to detail and focus 

 Good project / change management skills

titleByLocale.en: Graduate programme London 2026, Client Strategy & Assistant Relationship Manager
titleByLocale.it: Graduate programme London 2026, Client Strategy & Assistant Relationship Manager
titleByLocale.de: Graduate programme London 2026, Client Strategy & Assistent Relationship Manager
titleByLocale.fr: Graduate programme London 2026, Client Strategy & Assistant Relationship Manager

jobs.description:
Job Description 
 
 This unique 12-month graduate program is based in London and offers a permanent contract. The exceptional opportunity for a recent master’s graduate is designed to provide in-depth, practical exposure to various areas of the business within a renowned Private Bank. Created as a comprehensive learning experience, enabling you to gain valuable insights into the finance industry and develop essential skills for a successful career in private banking. 
 
 The program is structured around two rotations of 6 months – in the CSO (Client Strategy Office) team and Banker’s support as Assistant Relationship Manager – after which the candidate will join the bank on a permanent basis. 
 This program is specifically focused on young graduates holding a master’s degree, with a maximum of 12 months of professional experience since graduation. Upon successful completion, you will have the opportunity to transition into a permanent role within one of the team, continuing your career with the bank and applying the knowledge, networks, and experience gained during the programme in a role aligned to your strengths and interests. 
 
 Role Description: 
 The role will have a broad remit: 
 In the CSO team the successful candidate will report to the Head of Client Strategy Office and will get exposure to Wealth Management Teams: You will work on 
 
 Sales & Performance: Build KPIs/dashboards, track banker metrics, manage pipeline, support budgeting, and prepare senior management materials. 

 Pricing & Strategy: Support on the pricing tool, ensure profitability, and provide insights; partner with Team leaders on client targeting and campaigns. 

 Project Management: Lead commercial initiatives, close audit points, ensure regulatory compliance, develop front-office tools, and track actions vs. the strategic plan. 

 
 As part of the ARMs team, the successful candidate will gain exposure to the full client relationship cycle. You will be working with the relationship managers on: Clients: Maintain relationships; be backup for RM; prep/run follow-ups. 
 Execution: Own cash management; execute/settle FX, cash, equities, FI. 
 Onboarding/Compliance: Open accounts end-to-end; deliver KYC/AML and reviews. 
 Credit: Prepare/administer loan and credit requests. 
 Growth: Spot cross-sell opportunities; support RM revenue. 
 Improvement: Streamline processes; keep product/market knowledge current. 
 
 

 
 Requirements: 
 Educational Background: Recent graduate ( master’s degree) in Finance, Business, Marketing, Media and PR, Economics or Engineering from a highly recognised university. 
 Work Experience : Relevant initial work experience in the finance industry (internship) is an advantage but not mandatory. 
 Technical Skills: Strong knowledge of MS Office tools – Excel, Word, Power Point. 
 Language Proficiency: Fluency in English is required. 
 Key Skills: 
 Excellent verbal and written communication skills 
 Ability to engage and collaborate with people from different business areas 
 Strong analytical and data skills- comfortable working with data and numbers 
 Strong problem-solving skills, results-driven attitude, proactive mindset 
 Structured approach 
 You have a natural passion to deliver great client outcomes 
 Strong business development aptitude with the ability to spot opportunities for business growth through existing client circumstances 
 ; 

 
 Work Authorization: Applicants must have the legal right to work in the UK at the time of application. We do not offer sponsorship. 
 
 **** 
 Applications Process: 
 We only accept online applications submitted through our official website. 
 Ensure that your application includes the following documents: 
 -CV 
 -Motivation Letter 
 -Grade Transcript & Diplomas 
 -Reference Letters 
 Key Dates: 
 ■ Programme start date: 1st September 2026 
 ■ Duration of programme: 12 months (permanent contract) 
 ■ Interviews Period: March to June 2026

jobs.title: Full-Stack Developer - Wealth Management
jobs.description:
Mission 
 The candidate will be part of the Data Intelligence & Digitalisation team within Wealth Management and will take part in the development of the digital platform for private bankers. The Wealth Management is currently enhancing its digital platform with the objectives to better integrate data and services for bankers and clients. The candidate will have to work in a complex environment involving technical and business challenges with many stakeholders. 
 Main responsibilities 
 
 Participate in the development effort of the digital platform 
 By integrating existing web services into a new user experience 
 Develop screens and navigation in the intranet Portal 
 Design and implement new features across the existing software stack 
 Define and challenge upcoming features from a technical perspective 
 Navigate through a large set of existing databases and write optimized queries against them 
 Work closely with business and quantitative analysts to ensure that technical solutions are feasible and meet the needs of the business 
 
 Your Profile 
 
 3 to 5 years experience
 Excellent computing and analytical skills. In particular, the ability to drill-down in existing code in the absence of any documentation 
 Proactive and user-focused, with a genuine interest in the functional as well as technical aspects of each challenge encountered 
 Full-stack development across .NET 8.0 (C#) and legacy .NET Framework applications, plus JavaScript (Vue.js) 
 Knowledge of web-based application development. Interest in UI/UX. Knowledge of PrimeVue and AG grid will be a plus 
 Broad experience of databases. SQL (Oracle) will be used as well as Entity Framework 
 Knowledge of continuous integration/continuous deployment (ideally with Azure Devops, Ansible, Jenkins and GIT) 
 The ability to adapt to and learn new skills and techniques in a very dynamic and changing environment 
 
 Nice to have 
 Worked in an Agile development environment with Jira 
 Knowledge of finance and banking is a plus 
 
 Others Swiss Residence 
 
 
 Core Competencies : Adherence to the company’s values: Dedication, Conviction, Agility and Responsibility - Compliance with regulations and internal directives

jobs.title: Business Monitoring & Control Officer (M/F)
jobs.description:
MISSION AND MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES : 
 The general role of Business Monitoring & Control (BMC) is to ensure that the operations in place are as effective and efficient as possible. This can involve reviewing the processes in place and assessing to determine possible ways of their improvement. 
 As a Business Monitoring & Control Officer (BMC), you will be responsible for implementing and maintaining robust control measures across various business functions. Your expertise will be instrumental in mitigating risks, ensuring compliance with regulations, and driving continuous improvement. You will collaborate closely with different departments, offering valuable insights and recommendations to enhance overall business performance and maintain our reputation as a trusted financial services provider. 
 BMC-officers consider in their work how to ensure that the business operates in a way that meets the needs of their customers effectively and in line with the governance of the Bank. They will control if the 1st line of defense duties has been correctly performed and will report on regular basis to the 2nd line of defense. 
 They form the link between the relationship managers (RMs) and the governing bodies (e.g., Management Committee and Board of Directors) and other units.

MISSION AND MAIN RESPONSIBILITIES : 
 The general role of Business Monitoring & Control (BMC) is to ensure that the operations in place are as effective and efficient as possible. This can involve reviewing the processes in place and assessing to determine possible ways of their improvement. 
 As a Business Monitoring & Control Officer (BMC), you will be responsible for implementing and maintaining robust control measures across various business functions. Your expertise will be instrumental in mitigating risks, ensuring compliance with regulations, and driving continuous improvement. You will collaborate closely with different departments, offering valuable insights and recommendations to enhance overall business performance and maintain our reputation as a trusted financial services provider.
